creative_brief: Split frame: left side shows a generic supermarket barcode on shrink-wrapped meat, cold and anonymous. Right side shows a hand-written farm name tag on butcher paper (e.g. 'Bundarra Farm, NSW') with warm, natural light. Clean typography overlays the if/then hook.
primary_text:
If you can name your barista but not a single farmer who feeds your family, something broke.

The food system buried them. Thousands of kilometres of cold chain, wholesalers, and barcodes sit between you and the person who grew your dinner.

FarmThru is a grocery store built to reverse that. Every product traces to a named farm. Bundarra pork from NSW. Wild-caught salmon from Collins on the South Coast. Paris Creek bio-dynamic dairy from South Australia.

No middlemen. No warehouses. No subscriptions. Order when you want, collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

This is becoming something bigger. You can own a piece of it.
headline: Name the farmer who fed you last
description: Farm-direct groceries from real producers. No lock-in, no middlemen.
